description_long: This is a rustic wooden fence composed of three thick vertical posts and a single horizontal rail that is unevenly attached, giving it a rugged, hand-built appearance. The wood is weathered, with visible grain, knots, and some discoloration, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. The posts are roughly cylindrical and set at irregular angles, while the rail is slightly bent and notched where it meets the posts. There are no decorative elements or caps on the posts, emphasizing a utilitarian, rural style. The surface texture is rough and matte, with a dark brown coloration and hints of greenish moss or lichen. This type of fence is commonly used to demarcate boundaries in parks, gardens, or along rural paths.
description: A rustic, weathered wooden fence with three posts and a single uneven rail, featuring a rough, natural finish and no decorative elements.
description_view_0: The fence is seen from the side, showing all three posts and the full length of the horizontal rail, highlighting its uneven attachment.
description_view_1: Viewed at an angle, the irregular spacing and alignment of the posts and the bent rail are more apparent.
description_view_2: This view emphasizes the length of the rail and the rough, natural texture of the wood, with the posts receding into the background.
description_view_3: From this angle, the thickness of the posts and the rugged, hand-built quality of the fence are most visible.
